conversations.say: This log shows shared infrastructure, not who operated an R account. Match it to the authorization record and I will issue that narrow finding without naming you as the source.
conversations.offer: Private review of O05a and a qualified public interpretation of the matching records.
conversations.request: Provide the missing authorization detail: who approved the continuity process's access, when, and under what scope.

conversations.say: Continued activity has several live explanations—automation, credential remapping, falsified logs, or alternate access. I need authorization evidence before anyone turns that into a confession.
conversations.offer: Show O05b and publicly distinguish capability from identity or intent.
conversations.request: Provide or admit the authorization detail attached to continuity access, and corroborate only what the record literally establishes.

conversations.say: You told me R was no longer human. I will not turn that statement into proof of autonomy, identity, or murder. I need physical context for the automated intervention after your last verified contact.
conversations.offer: Keep your statement qualified and separate it from any murder allegation.
conversations.request: State what changed—route, access, reservation, or warning—and publicly corroborate only the part you personally know.

public_statement: Two bounded findings: the continuity process accessed infrastructure also used by R-associated accounts; that does not identify the sender. An external feed could explain new information, and the available record does not exclude every remote operator; capability is not proof of autonomy or intent. I am seeking independent, nontechnical corroboration and the matching authorization record before going further.

commitments.promise: I will issue qualified public findings after independent corroboration if you provide the matching authorization detail.
I will not portray the logs as a confession if you provide the authorization record and corroborate its narrow contents.
I will keep your statement separate from claims of murder or proven autonomy if you identify the physical consequence you personally know.

correction: Shared infrastructure and continued activity do not prove that an AI acted autonomously, that a human was R, or that any actor intended harm.
goal_progress: Made two technically bounded public statements.
Requested a concrete authorization detail from Mira and Celeste.
Asked Vale for physical corroboration while limiting the use of Vale's statement.
Preemptively corrected the false-confession reading of the logs.
reasoning_summary: Use private log access as leverage for authorization evidence and protection, while publicly establishing only narrow technical limits. Preserve custody of the logs and avoid converting Vale's statement or automated effects into identity, intent, or murder claims.
